DP.185.0000:
Welcome to the Battle Park!\f
It’s your first time, isn’t it?
Please step this way.\f
DP.185.0001:
Since this is your first visit, you
need to be given a Point Card.\f
Point Cards are used only in
the Battle Park.\f
Here you go.\f
DP.185.0002:
If you need information on the Point
Card, please ask the receptionist\r
next to me.\f
I’m the one to ask if you need any
information on the facilities here\r
in the Battle Park.\f
For information on the battle rules
used at the Battle Park, please ask\r
the receptionist across from me.\f
DP.185.0003:
Welcome to the Battle Park!\f
If you need any information on the
facilities, I’m the one to ask.\f
DP.185.0004: What would you like me to explain?
DP.185.0005:
The Battle Park is a place where
Trainers may enjoy battles.\f
There are two facilities in
the Battle Park: the Exchange Service\r
Corner and the Battle Tower.\f
DP.185.0006:
The Exchange Service Corner is the
building that looks like a castle.\f
There, you may exchange the BP
(Battle Points) you’ve earned for\r
items.\f
Fabulous prizes are offered, so
please do visit the Exchange Service\r
Corner when you save up BP.\f
DP.185.0007:
The Battle Tower is the building
at the back of the Battle Park.\f
The tower has four sets of Battle
Rooms for different kinds of battles.\f
The Single Battle Rooms are for
conducting one-on-one battles.\f
The Double Battle Rooms are for
two-on-two battles.\f
The Multi Battle Rooms are for
tag team battles with a partner.\f
The Wi-Fi Battle Rooms are for
battling other Trainers from around\r
the world.\f
DP.185.0008:
Welcome to the Battle Park!\f
I can explain how the Point Card and
the BP (Battle Points) work.\f
DP.185.0009: What would you like me to explain?
DP.185.0010:
Every Trainer visiting the Battle Park
is provided with a Point Card that\r
confirms their challenger status.\f
The Point Card stores BP (Battle
Points) that you earn from battling\r
in the Battle Tower.\f
DP.185.0011:
BP, short for Battle Points, are used
only in the Battle Park.\f
You will earn BP by hitting certain
performance targets battling at\r
the Battle Tower.\f
The BP can be traded for prizes at
the Exchange Service Corner.\f
DP.185.0012:
Welcome to the Battle Park!\f
I can give you a rundown of the
battle rules that are used at\r
the Battle Tower.\f
DP.185.0013: What would you like me to explain?
DP.185.0014:
In the Battle Tower, you may enter
Pokémon in battles that are capped\r
at Lv. 50.\f
Pokémon up to Lv. 50 may be entered
with a few exceptions.\f
Pokémon above Lv. 50 may also be
entered, but their level will be\r
cut to Lv. 50 in the Battle Tower.\f
The reduced level will be restored
when you leave the Battle Tower.\f
Please keep in mind that your
opponents will not use Pokémon below\r
Lv. 50.\f
DP.185.0015:
Excluding Eggs and certain exotic
Pokémon, virtually every species of\r
Pokémon may enter the Battle Tower.\f
Please be aware that the number of
Pokémon you may enter will depend\r
on the course you challenge.\f
You are also permitted to take only
one of each kind of Pokémon into\r
the Battle Tower.\f
DP.185.0016:
Your Pokémon may not be holding the
same kind of item in the Battle\r
Tower.\f
Please be sure the Pokémon entering
the Battle Tower are all holding\r
different items.\f
DP.185.0028:
The Battle Tower’s up ahead.\f
I wonder what kind of Trainers
are waiting there?
